Enjoy this clip and I'll see you again on Monday tomorrow. You get another clip. Relaxing Sunday clip, but I'll see you Monday for back to work daily motivations slash skills training. I have a real estate sales firm in Perth. My name is Simon and I'd like your input regarding attracting salespeople to a company. They're a unique breed. They are that. You know, there's two ways to go about it with salesmen. One is to poach from other sales company, all the real estate companies and the other is to home grow your own salespeople. I have always been a believer that I wanted to home grow my own people. That being said, I don't know enough about your business to say, do you have in your company right now a sales training program where you can take, is there something in place to take a guy? Yes, we do. We have a cadet system we take on young guys. Bravo. Bravo too. Okay, so I would certainly focus on taking people and you know, that I don't like to take poach because you know when you poach from other people, you have all hired guns and there's just no loyalty and it becomes, it can very quickly spiral out of control. So what's the question? Where do you find them or what's the, give me the more precise aspect of this? Well, firstly, I want to know everything, but where do you find potential people, qualities look for retaining good salespeople? Generally there's a, between agencies, there's a race around commission, you know, people offer another 5% or a referral system or some advantage, but rarely, it rarely is it sincere. Right. Okay, so number one, again, I sort of answered this question before in terms of if you, there's two aspects. So have you been advertising for salespeople when they come in and you're not sure who to hire or you're advertising and no one's showing up? We advertise on seek. We're not getting quality of applicant. Okay, so you're not getting a quality of applicant online, okay? And have you thought of recruiting out of the colleges? People that are graduating from college is one way that you go about finding young, hungry salespeople. The real estate institute of Western Australia has regular courses. We could stalk these people as they come out for lunch, which is feasible by the way, I would do it. But the, yeah, I'm up for it if I think it would work. Well, listen, here's the thing, okay? Again, I don't know the local, like see every, one, let's just say Lee over here, Lee had an issue where he was in a certain part of Brisbane and was finding it really, really hard to recruit people. They just didn't, where he was in the central district, there was not good help around. He actually very smartly moved his company to a different part outside the suburbs and now people are pouring into work there. Sometimes it's where your office is strategically located that makes it attractive to salespeople. There's lots of variables involved and what is not causing me to be able to hire people. One question I have is, what else is it just seekers? Any place you advertise it's just seek. We watch other people, as salespeople enter the industry, the industry logs their performance. You get rising stars, newbies, the rookie awards and all that sort of stuff. We watch those people. If they're in a geographic area that is attractive to us, we'll start a dialogue with them. It's not effective so far. Yeah, listen, here's the deal. In terms of what you could do to attract salespeople is the environment that you create, in the sort of some team building stuff I'm going to talk about tomorrow here, you create an environment where once you get people that they want to stay and you get compensation plans that tie people in. For instance, I don't know what the rules are in real estate, but in lots of industries you give people X amount and then there's some deferred compensation that they have to stay for that invests over time. You can give people golden handcuffs so they can't leave you so easily. It's really, really effective in retaining staff. What I would do is honestly, I would actually, with me, I'd find out how my competition, I'd say who in my industry seems to be recruiting and hiring people with a fast clip. Is anybody out there doing it and getting lots of new people? Yes, there's two agencies. How are they doing it? Offering cash incentives to come across, like a fat check. Okay, well that's just stealing other people's agents, right? Well, you have to ask yourself if you want to be in that business. If you want to, is that a model you want or not? If it's not a model you want, then I wouldn't go after that. Is there any other body who's making big progress in hiring but not doing that, not stealing people? I would say no. So no one right now has the ability to go out there and hire a lot of people, is that what you're saying? Yet I know Perth pretty well and there's tons of young people there looking for jobs. We're in the top end of town, so I can't employ kids. What's that? I'm in the top end of town. Anyone who's 30 years old is a kid to me. I'm not talking 12-year-olds. I'm taking 25, 30, 30. When I say kid, I just mean someone that's under 40. It's a generic. Here's what I would do.
